codeReview.summary: The commit adds envelope/dispatcher service code and broad tests, but the runtime flow does not actually use the dispatcher path and does not enforce the no-envelope guard in executeRun. There are also company-scope violations in new dispatcher queries.

issues.description: Dispatcher flow is not integrated into production dispatch: scheduler.processIntent still resolves workspace and creates execution_envelopes directly instead of calling dispatcherService.dispatchRun(). This bypasses the feature’s intended dispatcher/envelope flow and makes dispatcher-specific rejection/event behavior non-authoritative.

executeRun still performs inline workspace/context resolution (resolveWorkspaceForRun) rather than consuming a pre-constructed envelope as the source of dispatch context, so requirement (3) in the feature description is not implemented end-to-end.

Runs without envelopes are not blocked. executeRun only conditionally injects envelope context when run.envelopeId exists and otherwise continues execution; dispatcher.requireEnvelopeForRun() is never invoked by the runtime path.

Company-scoped invariant is violated in new dispatcher queries: explicit workspace lookup (.where(eq(projectWorkspaces.id, input.workspaceId))) and issue lookup (.where(eq(issues.id, input.issueId))) omit companyId filters, enabling cross-company data access risk.

VAL-HARD-031’s stated evidence expects an attempted envelope update to fail, but the immutability test does not attempt an update and instead asserts API shape/comments. Coverage is useful but does not directly exercise update rejection semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure-adherence signaling is weak: backend-worker requires test-first red/green, but this run implemented services before tests and first targeted test execution was already green while handoff.skillFeedback still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:32-41 requires red phase; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 5b71292b-3957-4dfe-b598-b0612477c566 shows Create envelope.ts/dispatcher.ts before test execution, and first vitest run reports all 26 tests passing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker skill does not explicitly restate the company-scope query rule, which appears to be an error-prone gap for service implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md:29 states all queries must include companyId; dispatcher.ts:96 and dispatcher.ts:112 introduce unscoped lookups in a newly implemented service.

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, commit c6681a77 diff, transcript skeleton, backend-worker skill, mission AGENTS, services.yaml, and library architecture notes. Result is fail with four blocking issues: dispatcher path not wired into runtime flow, executeRun still does inline context resolution, no-envelope runs are not blocked, and dispatcher has company-scope query violations.
